_doc: Asset-tagging configuration for SecurityInsight. Copy this file to 'asset-tagging.config.json' in the same directory (gitignored) and customize. The tagging engine reads the JSON at startup. Two kinds of customisation live here: (1) per-rule on/off gates via 'AutoExcludeRules.*'; (2) device-name filter patterns that any rule can opt-in to via the YAML field 'ApplyDeviceNamePatterns: true' instead of hard-coding the filter inside KQL.

AutoExcludeRules._doc: Auto-exclusion rules: each flag, when true, makes the tagging engine emit an MDE machine tag of '<bucket>--Excluded--SI' on every device whose Defender XDR OnboardingStatus matches the bucket. The existing Risk Analysis + asset-profiling exclusion machinery (RiskAnalysisGlobalExclusions.custom.json's ExcludedAssetTags: ['--Excluded--SI']) then drops those devices from every RA report and from every profiling rule automatically.
AutoExcludeRules._ExcludeCanBeOnboardedDoc: When true, tag every device with DeviceInfo.OnboardingStatus = 'Can be onboarded' (Defender sees the device but Sense agent isn't installed yet). Use during onboarding rollouts to silence in-flight devices.
AutoExcludeRules._ExcludeInsufficientInfoDoc: When true, tag every device with OnboardingStatus = 'Insufficient info' (Defender has signal traces but not enough to identify the device class).
AutoExcludeRules._ExcludeUnsupportedDoc: When true, tag every device with OnboardingStatus = 'Unsupported' (legacy / appliance OS Defender can't onboard at all).

DeviceNamePatterns._doc: Generic device-name filter set. Any tagging rule that opts in via the YAML field 'ApplyDeviceNamePatterns: true' has the engine filter its post-KQL row set against these patterns BEFORE writing tags. When ALL three arrays are empty, opt-in rules effectively get 'match every device' (no filter). When any one is populated, a device matches if its DeviceName satisfies ANY entry across ANY array (OR semantics). Lives here so a customer never has to edit KQL to change which boxes the auto-exclude rules apply to.
DeviceNamePatterns._StartsWithDoc: Case-insensitive prefix match. Example: ['test-', 'lab-', 'qa-'] tags only devices whose name starts with one of these prefixes.
